summary: The Sprint 8 changes are generally correct. The analytics module split preserves existing logic faithfully. Rate limiting additions are straightforward and correct. The main correctness concern is in analytics_fx.py's _nearest_fx_rate function which has an index-to-candidate mapping ambiguity that could return the wrong rate in edge cases. The journal empty state logic has a potential condition gap. Financial calculations (fx gain/loss, CAGR, Sharpe, MDD) are mathematically sound with appropriate zero-division guards.

findings.title: _nearest_fx_rate candidate selection has off-by-one ambiguity
findings.detail: candidates[0] is appended from fx_dates_sorted[idx] (next), candidates[1] from fx_dates_sorted[idx-1] (prev). Then d0 is computed as distance to idx-1 (prev) and d1 as distance to idx (next). The return is 'candidates[1] if d0 <= d1 else candidates[0]', meaning when prev is closer (d0 <= d1), it returns candidates[1] which IS the prev rate — that's correct. However when idx == len(fx_dates_sorted) (target_date is after all snapshots), idx < len is false so only candidates[1] (prev) is appended as candidates[0], and the single-candidate branch returns candidates[0], which is correct. Edge case: when idx==0 (target before all snapshots), only candidates[0] (next) is appended, which is correct. The logic appears to work but the reversed index labeling makes it extremely hard to verify and is a maintenance trap.
findings.fix: Rename variables for clarity: next_rate and prev_rate. Compute distance to each directly and return the closer one.

findings.title: invalidate_analytics_cache misses portfolio-specific cache keys
findings.detail: get_portfolio_history now creates portfolio_id-specific cache keys like 'analytics:{uid}:portfolio-history:1M:42'. The invalidate_analytics_cache function only deletes keys like 'portfolio-history:1M' (without portfolio_id suffix). After a sync, portfolio-specific compare page history caches will remain stale.
findings.fix: Either use Redis pattern-based deletion (SCAN + DEL 'analytics:{uid}:portfolio-history:*') or accept stale data until TTL expires. The simplest fix is to document the limitation, since the TTL is 1 hour and invalidation of the aggregate key already occurs.

findings.title: Sharpe ratio uses population variance instead of sample variance
findings.detail: The variance is calculated as sum((r - mean)**2) / n (population variance). For financial Sharpe ratio calculation, sample variance (/ (n-1)) is the convention when estimating from historical data. This results in a slightly biased Sharpe estimate, overstating it for small sample sizes.
findings.fix: Use / (n - 1) for variance when n > 1, consistent with standard financial computation.

findings.title: portfolio-history cache key includes portfolio_id but invalidation does not
findings.detail: cache_suffix includes portfolio_id when present (e.g. 'portfolio-history:3M:42') but invalidate_analytics_cache only deletes 'portfolio-history:3M'. This is a duplicate description of COR-002 for completeness — the same root cause.
